author: John Chrysostom

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “If therefore perfection were by the Levitical priesthood; (for under it the people have received the law’ ) what further need was there that another priest should arise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the priesthood being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For He of whom these things are spoken, pertained to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is evident that our Lord sprang out of Judah, of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priests.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “If therefore” (he says) “perfection were by the Levitical priesthood.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Having spoken concerning Melchisedec, and shown how much superior he was to Abraham, and having set forth the great difference between them, he begins from this point forward to prove the wide difference as to the covenant itself, and how the one is imperfect and the other perfect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: However he does not even yet enter on the matters themselves, but first contends on the ground of the priesthood, and the tabernacle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For these things would be more easily received by the unbelieving, when the proof was derived from things already allowed, and believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He had shown that Melchisedec was greatly superior both to Levi and to Abraham, being to them in the rank of the priests.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Again he argues from a different point.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What then is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why (he says) did he not say, “after the order of Aaron”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And observe, I pray you, the great superiority [of his argument].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For from the very circumstance which naturally excluded His priesthood, viz. that He was not “after the order of Aaron,” from that he establishes Him, and excludes the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this is the very thing that I say (he declares); why has He “not been made after the order of Aaron”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And the [saying] “what further need” has much emphasis.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if Christ had been “after the order of Melchisedec” according to the flesh, and then afterwards the law had been introduced, and all that pertained to Aaron, one might reasonably say that the latter as being more perfect, annulled the former, seeing that it had come in after it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if Christ comes later, and takes a different type, as that of His priesthood, it is evident that it is because those were imperfect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For (he would say) let us suppose for argument’s sake, that all has been fulfilled, and that there is nothing imperfect in the priesthood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “What need” was there in that case that He should be called “after the order of Melchisedec and not after the order of Aaron”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why did He set aside Aaron, and introduce a different priesthood, that of Melchisedec?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “If then perfection,” that is the perfection of the things themselves, of the doctrines, of life, “had been by the Levitical priesthood.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And observe how he goes forward on his path.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He had said that [He was] “after the order of Melchisedec,” implying that the [priesthood] “after the order of Melchisedec” is superior: for [he was] far superior.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Afterwards he shows this from the time also, in that He was after Aaron; evidently as being better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what is the meaning of what follows?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For” (he says) “under [or “upon”] it the people have received the Law [or “have been legislated for”].”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is “under it” [&c.]?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Ordereth itself by it; through it does all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: You cannot say that it was given to others, “the people under it have received the law,” that is, have used it, and did use it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: You cannot say indeed that it was perfect, it did not govern the people; “they have been legislated for upon it,” that is, they used it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What need was there then of another priesthood?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For the priesthood being changed, there is of necessity a change of the law also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if there must be another priest, or rather another priesthood, there must needs be also another law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is for those who say, What need was there of a new Covenant?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he could indeed have alleged a testimony from prophecy also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “This is the covenant which I made with your fathers” [&c.]. ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But for the present he contends on the ground of the priesthood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And observe, how he says this from the first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said, “According to the order of Melchisedec.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By this he excluded the order of Aaron.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he would not have said “After the order of Melchisedec,” if the other had been better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If therefore another priesthood has been brought in, there must be also [another] Covenant; for neither is it possible that there should be a priest, without a covenant and laws and ordinances, nor that having received a different priesthood He should use the former [covenant].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In the next place, as to the ground of objection: “How could He be a priest if He were not a Levite?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Having overthrown this by what had been said above, he does not even think it worth answering, but introduces it in passing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I said (he means) that the priesthood was changed, therefore also the Covenant is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And it was changed not only in its character, or in its ordinances, but also in its tribe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For of necessity [it must be changed] in its tribe also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For the priesthood being changed [or “transferred”],” from tribe to tribe, from the sacerdotal to the regal [tribe], that the same might be both regal and sacerdotal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And observe the mystery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: First it was royal, and then it is become sacerdotal: so therefore also in regard to Christ: for King indeed He always was, but has become Priest from the time that He assumed the Flesh, that He offered the sacrifice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest the change, and the very things which were ground of objection these he introduces, as though the natural order of things required them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For” (he says) “He of whom these things are spoken pertained to another tribe.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I myself also say it, I know that this tribe [of Judah] had nothing of priesthood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For there is a transferring.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yea and I am showing another difference also (he would say): not only from the tribe, nor yet only from the Person, nor from the character [of the Priesthood], nor from the covenant, but also from the type itself. ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 16 ) “Who was made [“became” so], not according to the law of a carnal commandment, but according to the power of an endless life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He became” (he says) “a priest not according to the law of a carnal commandment”: for that law was in many respects unlawful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is, “of a carnal commandment”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Circumcise the flesh, it says; anoint the flesh; wash the flesh; purify the flesh; shave the flesh; bind upon the flesh; cherish the flesh; rest as to the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again its blessings, what are they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Long life for the flesh; milk and honey for the flesh; peace for the flesh; luxury for the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: From this law Aaron received the priesthood; Melchisedec however not so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And it is yet far more evident, if after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is evident?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The interval between the two priesthoods, the difference; how much superior He is “who was made not according to the law of a carnal commandment.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Melchisedec?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nay; but Christ.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But according to the power of an endless life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For He testifieth, Thou art a Priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec”; that is, not for a time, nor having any limit, “but according to the power of an endless life,” that is, by means of power, by means of “endless life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And yet this does not follow after, “who was made not according to the law of a carnal commandment”: for what would follow would be to say, “but according to that of a spiritual one.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: However by “carnal,” he implied temporary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: As he says also in another place, carnal ordinances imposed until the time of reformation.” ( c. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “According to the power of life,” that is, because He lives by His own power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He had said, that there is also a change of law, and up to this point he has shown it; henceforward he enquires into the cause, that which above all gives full assurance to men’s minds, [I mean] the knowing the cause thoroughly; and it leads us more to faith when we have learned also the cause, and the principle according to which [the thing] comes to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For there is verily” (he says) “a disannulling of the commandment going before, for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here the Heretics press on.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But listen attentively.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He did not say “for the evil,” nor, “for the viciousness,” but “for the weakness and unprofitableness [thereof],” yea and in other places also he shows the weakness; as when he says “In that it was weak through the flesh.” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: [The law] itself then is not weak, but we.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For the Law made nothing perfect.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is, “make nothing perfect”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Made no man perfect, being disobeyed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And besides, even if it had been listened to, it would not have made one perfect and virtuous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But as yet he does not say this here, but that it had no strength: and with good reason.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For written precepts were there set down, Do this and Do not that, being enjoined only, and not giving power within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But “the Hope” is not such.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is “a disannulling”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: A casting out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: A “disannulling” is a disannulling of things which are of force.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that he implied, that it [once] was of force, but henceforward was of no account, since it accomplished nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Was the Law then of no use?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It was indeed of use; and of great use: but to make men perfect it was of no use.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For in this respect he says, “The Law made nothing perfect.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: All were figures, all shadows; circumcision, sacrifice, sabbath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There fore they could not reach through the soul, wherefore they pass away and gradually withdraw.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But the bringing in of a better hope did, by which we draw nigh unto God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 20 ) “And forasmuch as not without the taking of an oath.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that the matter of the oath becomes necessary for him here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Accordingly for this reason he previously treated much [hereon], how that God swore; and swore for the sake of [our] fuller assurance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But the bringing in of a better hope.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that system also had a hope, but not such as this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For they hoped that, if they were well pleasing [to God], they should possess the land, that they should suffer nothing fearful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But in this [dispensation] we hope that, if we are well pleasing [to God], we shall possess not earth, but heaven; or rather (which is far better than this) we hope to stand near to God, to come unto the very throne of the Father, to minister unto Him with the Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And see how he introduces these things by little and little.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For above he says “which entereth into that within the veil”, ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 19 ), but here, “by which we draw nigh unto God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And inasmuch as not without an oath.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is “And inasmuch as not without an oath”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That is, Behold another difference also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And these things were not merely promised (he says).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For those priests were made without an oath,” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 21, 22 ) “but This with an oath, by Him that said unto Him, The Lord swore and will not repent, Thou art Priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better covenant.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He lays down two points of difference, that it hath no end as the [covenant] of the Law had; and this he proves from [its being] Christ who exercises [the priesthood]; for he says “according to the power of an endless life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he proves it also from the oath, because “He swore,” &c., and from the fact; for if the other was cast out, because it was weak, this stands firm, because it is powerful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He proves it also from the priest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because He is One [only]; and there would not have been One [only], unless He had been immortal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as there were many priests, because they were mortal, so [here is] The One, because He is immortal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better covenant,” inasmuch as He sware to Him that He should always be [Priest]; which He would not have done, if He were not living.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 25 ) “Wherefore He is able also to save them to the uttermost, that come unto God by Him, seeing He ever liveth to make intercession for them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that he says this in respect of that which is according to the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when He [appears] as Priest, then He also intercedes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore also when Paul says, “who also maketh intercession for us” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 34 ), he hints the same thing; the High Priest maketh intercession.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For He “that raiseth the dead as He will, and quickeneth them,” ( John v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 21 ), and that “even as the Father” [doth], how [is it that] when there is need to save, He “maketh intercession”? ( John v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He that hath “all judgment,” how [is it that] He “maketh intercession”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He that “sendeth His angels” ( Matt. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 41, 42 ), that they may “cast” some into “the furnace,” and save others, how [is it that] He “maketh intercession”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore (he says) “He is able also to save.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this cause then He saves, because He dies not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Inasmuch as “He ever liveth,” He hath (he means) no successor: And if He have no successor, He is able to aid all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For there [under the Law] indeed, the High Priest although he were worthy of admiration during the time in which he was [High Priest] (as Samuel for instance, and any other such), but, after this, no longer; for they were dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But here it is not so, but “He” saves “to the uttermost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is “to the uttermost”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He hints at some mystery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not here only (he says) but there also He saves them that “come unto God by Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How does He save?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “In that He ever liveth” (he says) “to make intercession for them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest the humiliation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest the manhood?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he says not, that He obtained this, by making intercession once for all, but continually, and whensoever it may be needful to intercede for them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “To the uttermost.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not for a time only, but there also in the future life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: ‘Does He then always need to pray?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yet how can [this] be reasonable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Even righteous men have oftentimes accomplished all by one entreaty, and is He always praying?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why then is He throned with [the Father]?’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that it is a condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The meaning is: Be not afraid, nor say, Yea, He loves us indeed, and He has confidence towards the Father, but He cannot live always.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For He doth live alway.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 26 ) “For such an High Priest also became us, who is holy, harmless, unde filed, separate from the sinners.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that the whole is said with reference to the manhood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (But when I say ‘the manhood,’ I mean [the manhood] having Godhead; not dividing [one from the other], but leaving [you] to suppose what is suitable.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Didst thou mark the difference of the High Priest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He has summed up what was said before, “in all points tempted like as we are yet without sin.” ( c. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For” (he says) “such an High Priest also became us, who is holy, harmless.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Harmless”: what is it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Without wickedness: that which another Prophet says: “guile was not found in His mouth” ( Isa. liii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 9 ), that is, [He is] not crafty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Could any one say this concerning God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And is one not ashamed to say that God is not crafty, nor deceitful?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Concerning Him, however, in respect of the Flesh, it might be reasonable [to say it].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Holy, undefiled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This too would any one say concerning God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For has He a nature capable of defilement?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Separate from sinners.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Does then this alone show the difference, or does the sacrifice itself also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 27 ) “He needeth not” (he says) “daily, as the High Priest, to offer up sacrifices for his sins, for this He did once for all, when He offered up Himself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “This,” what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Here what follows sounds a prelude concerning the exceeding greatness of the spiritual sacrifice and the interval [between them].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He has mentioned the point of the priest; he has mentioned that of the faith; he has mentioned that of the Covenant; not entirely indeed, still he has mentioned it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: In this place what follows is a prelude concerning the sacrifice itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do not then, having heard that He is a priest, suppose that He is always executing the priest’s office.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For He executed it once, and thenceforward “sat down.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Lest thou suppose that He is standing on high, and is a minister, he shows that the matter is [part] of a dispensation [or economy].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as He became a servant, so also [He became] a Priest and a Minister.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But as after becoming a servant, He did not continue a servant, so also, having become a Minister, He did not continue a Minister.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it belongs not to a minister to sit, but to stand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This then he hints at here, and also the greatness of the sacrifice, if being [but] one, and having been offered up once only, it affected that which all [the rest] were unable to do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But he does not yet [treat] of these points.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For this He did,” he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “This”; what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For” (he says) “it is of necessity that this [Man] have somewhat also to offer” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 3 ); not for Himself; for how did He offer Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But for the people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What sayest thou?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And is He able to do this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Yea (he says).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For the Law maketh men high priests, which have infirmity.” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And doth He not need to offer for Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: No, he says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, that you may not suppose that the [words, “this”] “He did once for all,” are said respecting Himself also, hear what he says: “For the law maketh men high priests, which have infirmity.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: On this account they both offer continually, and for themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He however who is mighty, He that hath no sin, why should He offer for Himself, or oftentimes for others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “But the word of the oath which was since the Law [maketh] the Son who has been consecrated for evermore.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Consecrated”: what is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Paul does not set down the common terms of contradistinction; for after saying “having Infirmity,” he did not say “the Son” who is mighty, but “consecrated”: i.e. mighty, as one might say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that the name Son is used in contradistinction to that of servant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And by “infirmity” he means either sin or death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is, “for evermore”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not now only without sin but always.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If then He is perfect, if He never sins, if He lives always, why shall He offer many sacrifices for us?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But for the present he does not insist strongly on this point: but what he does strongly insist upon is, His not offering on His own behalf.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Since then we have such an High Priest, let us imitate Him: let us walk in His footsteps.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There is no other sacrifice: one alone has cleansed us, and after this, fire and hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For indeed on this account he repeats it over and over, saying, “one Priest,” “one Sacrifice,” lest any one supposing that there are many [sacrifices] should sin without fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then, as many as have been counted worthy of The Seal, as many as have enjoyed The Sacrifice, as many as have partaken of the immortal Table, continue to guard our noble birth and our dignity for falling away is not without danger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And as many as have not yet been counted worthy these [privileges], let not these either be confident on that account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when a person goes on in sin, with the view of receiving holy baptism at the last gasp, oftentimes he will not obtain it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And, believe me, it is not to terrify you that I say what I am going to say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I have myself known many persons, to whom this has happened, who in expectation indeed of the enlightening sinned much, and on the day of their death went away empty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For God gave us baptism for this cause, that He might do away our sins, not that He might increase our sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Whereas if any man have employed it as a security for sinning more, it becomes a cause of negligence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if there had been no Washing, they would have lived more warily, as not having [the means of] forgiveness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou seest that we are the ones who cause it to be said “Let us do evil, that good may come.” ( Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore, I exhort you also who are uninitiated, be sober.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let no man follow after virtue as an hireling, no man as a senseless person, no man as after a heavy and burdensome thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us pursue it then with a ready mind, and with joy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if there were no reward laid up, ought we not to be good?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But however, at least with a reward, let us become good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And how is this anything else than a disgrace and a very great condemnation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Unless thou give me a reward (says one), I do not become self-controlled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Then am I bold to say something: thou wilt never be self-controlled, no not even when thou livest with self-control, if thou dost it for a reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou esteemest not virtue at all, if thou dost not love it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But on account of our great weakness, God was willing that for a time it should be practiced even for reward, yet not even so do we pursue it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But let us suppose, if you will, that a man dies, after having done innumerable evil things, having also been counted worthy of baptism (which however I think does not readily happen), tell me, how will he depart thither?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not indeed called to account for the deeds he had done, but yet without confidence; as is reasonable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when after living a hundred years, he has no good work to show, but only that he has not sinned, or rather not even this, but that he was saved by grace only, and when he sees others crowned, in splendor, and highly approved: even if he fall not into hell, tell me, will he endure his despondency?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But to make the matter clear by an example, Suppose there are two soldiers, and that one of them steals, injures, overreaches, and that the other does none of these things, but acts the part of a brave man, does important things well, sets up trophies in war, stains his right hand with blood; then when the time arrives, suppose that (from the same rank in which the thief also was) he is at once conducted to the imperial throne and the purple; but suppose that the other remains there where he was, and merely of the royal kindness does not pay the penalty of his deeds, let him however be in the last place, and let him be stationed under the King.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Tell me, will he be able to endure his despair when he sees him who was [ranked] with himself ascended even to the very highest dignities, and made thus glorious, and master of the world, while he himself still remains below, and has not even been freed from punishment with honor, but through the grace and kindness of the King?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For even should the King forgive him, and release him from the charges against him, still he will live in shame; for surely not even will others admire him: since in such forgiveness, we admire not those who receive the gifts, but those who bestow them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And as much as the gifts are greater, so much the more are they ashamed who receive them, when their transgressions are great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: With what eyes then will such an one be able to look on those who are in the King’s courts, when they exhibit their sweatings out of number and their wounds, whilst he has nothing to show, but has his salvation itself of the mere loving-kindness of God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as if one were to beg off a murderer, a thief, an adulterer, when he was going to be arrested, and were to command him to stay at the porch of the King’s palace, he will not afterwards be able to look any man in the face, although he has been set free from punishment: so too surely is this man’s case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For do not, I beseech you, suppose that because it is called a palace, therefore all attain the same things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if here in Kings’ courts there is the Prefect, and all who are about the King, and also those who are in very inferior stations, and occupy the place of what are called Decani (though the interval be so great between the Prefect and the Decanus) much more shall this be so in the royal court above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And this I say not of myself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For Paul layeth down another difference greater even than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For (he says) as many differences as there are between the sun and the moon and the stars and the very smallest star, so many also between those in the kingdom [of Heaven].

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And that the difference between the sun and the smallest star is far greater than that between the Decanus (as he is called) and the Prefect, is evident to all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For while the sun shines upon all the world at once, and makes it bright, and hides the moon and the stars, the other often does not appear, not even in the dark.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For there are many of the stars which we do not see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When then we see others become suns, and we have the rank of the very smallest stars, which are not even visible, what comfort shall we have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not, I beseech you, let us not be so slothful, not so inert, let us not barter away the salvation of God for an easy life, but let us make merchandise of it, and increase it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For even if one be a Catechumen, still he knows Christ, still he understands the Faith, still he is a hearer of the divine oracles, still he is not far from the knowledge; he knows the will of his Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wherefore does he procrastinate? wherefore does he delay and postpone?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nothing is better than a good life whether here or there, whether in case of the Enlightened or of the Catechumens,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For tell me what burdensome command have we enjoined?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Have a wife (it is said) and be chaste.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is this difficult?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How? when many, not Christians only but heathens also, live chastely without a wife.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That which the heathen surpasses for vainglory, thou dost not even keep for the fear of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Give (He says) to the poor out of what thou hast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is this burdensome?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But in this case also heathen condemn us who for vainglory only have emptied out their whole possessions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Use not filthy communication.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is this difficult?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if it had not been enjoined, ought we not to have done right in this, to avoid appearing degraded?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that the contrary conduct is troublesome, I mean the using filthy communication, is manifest from the fact that the soul is ashamed and blushes if it have been led to say any such thing and would not unless perhaps it were drunk.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when sitting in a public place, even if thou doest it at home, why dost thou not do it there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because of those that are present.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why dost thou not readily do the same thing before thy wife?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That thou mayest not insult her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So then thou dost it not, lest thou shouldest insult thy wife; and dost thou not blush at insulting God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For He is everywhere present, and heareth all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Be not drunken, He says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this very thing of itself, is it not a chastisement?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He did not say, Put thy body on the rack, but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do not give it free rein so as to take away the authority of the mind: on the contrary “make not provision for the lusts thereof.” ( Rom. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do not (He says) seize by violence what is not thine own; do not overreach; do not forswear thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What labors do these things require! what sweatings!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Speak evil of no man (He says) nor accuse falsely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The contrary indeed is a labor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For when thou hast spoken ill of another, immediately thou art in danger, in suspicion, [saying] Did he of whom I spake, hear? whether he be great or small.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For should he be a great man, immediately thou wilt be indeed in danger; but if small, he will requite thee with as much, or rather with what is far more grievous; for he will say evil of thee in a greater degree.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: We are enjoined nothing difficult, nothing burdensome, if we have the will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And if we have not the will, even the easiest things will appear burdensome to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What is easier than eating? but from great effeminacy many feel disgust even at this, and I hear many say, that it is weariness even to eat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: None of these things is wearisome if thou hast but the will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For everything depends on the will after the grace from above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us will good things that we may attain also to the good things eternal, in Christ Jesus our Lord, whom to the Father together with the Holy Ghost be glory, might, honor, now and for ever, and world without end.
